title: Inspect ops: info and sheet list
description: Implement ops/inspect.py with info() (returns file path, list of sheet names, list of named range names) and list_sheets() (returns per-sheet name, max_row, max_column, and visibility). Wire the 'pyxlsx info' and 'pyxlsx sheet list' CLI commands in cli.py.
acceptance_criteria: info() returns a dict with keys file, sheets (list of names), and named_ranges (list of names)
list_sheets() returns a list of dicts each with name, rows, cols, and visible
pyxlsx info <file> outputs JSON matching the spec and --plain produces the human-readable format
pyxlsx sheet list <file> outputs JSON matching the spec and --plain produces the tabular format
Errors are written to stderr and exit code is 1

handoff_summary.completed: info() returns {file, sheets, named_ranges} using openpyxl 3.1 DefinedNameDict API. list_sheets() returns {sheets: [{name, rows, cols, visible}]}. Both CLI commands work with JSON (default) and --plain output. Exit code 1 on errors via sys.exit(1). Fixed pyproject.toml packages path from ".apm/skills/pyxlsx/pyxlsx" to "pyxlsx" (required for the package to be installable from its own directory).
handoff_summary.remaining: Tests for inspect ops are the tester agent's responsibility (test_xlsx_inspect.py and test_xlsx_cli.py). Downstream beads cover the remaining commands: sheet read, table read, cell get, cell set, sheet add/delete/rename, unpack/pack.
handoff_summary.risks: The pyproject.toml fix changes scope slightly beyond this bead's two expected files — however it was a blocking bug (package not importable) and is within the same skill directory.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should run test_xlsx_inspect.py and test_xlsx_cli.py against pyxlsx info and sheet list commands.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: cli.py has two downstream beads adding more commands (sheet read, table read, cell get/set, etc.). Conflicts likely at the end of the file where new command groups/commands are appended. Keep the existing group stubs (sheet, table, cell) in place.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Used openpyxl 3.1 DefinedNameDict iteration (list(wb.defined_names)) rather than the legacy .definedName attribute list. Used read_only=True, data_only=True for both ops since they are pure read operations. Errors call sys.exit(1) after printing to stderr — callers catching SystemExit will see exit code 1.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Smoke-tested manually: info() and list_sheets() both return correct dicts; CLI commands produce correct JSON and --plain output; hidden sheet visibility flag works; named ranges round-trip correctly.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: openpyxl list(wb.defined_names) returns workbook-scope defined names only; sheet-scoped names are not included. This matches the spec wording ('named range names defined in the workbook') and the example output.

conflict_risks: cli.py has two downstream beads adding more commands (sheet read, table read, cell get/set, etc.). Conflicts likely at the end of the file where new command groups/commands are appended. Keep the existing group stubs (sheet, table, cell) in place.

execution_history.summary: Implemented ops/inspect.py with info() and list_sheets(), wired pyxlsx info and pyxlsx sheet list CLI commands in cli.py, and fixed a pyproject.toml packages path bug from the scaffold bead that prevented the package from being importable.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Python` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `python -m py_compile`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `pytest` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
